intracerebral_hemorrhage.title: Hematoma Expansion following Intracerebral Hemorrhage: Mechanisms Targeting the Coagulation Cascade and Platelet Activation
intracerebral_hemorrhage.abstract: Intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH) is a devastating subtype of stroke with high mortality. Hematoma expansion is a key determinant of neurological deterioration. This review examines mechanisms targeting the coagulation cascade and platelet activation in ICH, including recombinant factor VIIa and platelet transfusion strategies.

intracerebral_hemorrhage.title: Collagenase-induced intracerebral hemorrhage in rats: behavioral and histological outcomes
intracerebral_hemorrhage.abstract: BACKGROUND: Animal models are essential for studying intracerebral hemorrhage. METHODS: We induced ICH in rats using collagenase injection into the striatum. RESULTS: Significant neurological deficits were observed at 24 hours. Hematoma volume correlated with behavioral scores. CONCLUSION: This model recapitulates key features of human ICH.

intracerebral_hemorrhage.title: Intracerebral hemorrhage and oral amphetamine.
intracerebral_hemorrhage.abstract: A case report of intracerebral hemorrhage associated with oral amphetamine use in a young adult.

distractors.title: Direct medical cost of stroke in Singapore
distractors.abstract: We analyze the economic burden of stroke including ischemic stroke and hemorrhagic stroke in Singapore healthcare system.

distractors.title: Acute myeloid leukaemia induced by mitoxantrone: case report
distractors.abstract: We report a case of acute myeloid leukaemia following mitoxantrone treatment for multiple sclerosis.

structured_abstract.title: Efficacy of blood pressure lowering in acute intracerebral hemorrhage: a meta-analysis
structured_abstract.abstract: BACKGROUND: Blood pressure management in acute intracerebral hemorrhage remains controversial. OBJECTIVE: To assess the efficacy of early blood pressure lowering in ICH. METHODS: We conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials. RESULTS: Early blood pressure lowering reduced hematoma expansion (OR 0.78, 95% CI 0.65-0.94). Mortality was not significantly different. CONCLUSION: Early blood pressure lowering is safe and reduces hematoma expansion.

missing_doi.title: Intracerebral hematoma volume measurement using open source software
missing_doi.abstract: We describe a method for measuring intracerebral hematoma volume using freely available software.
